thread.title: Elon Musk: India Counts 640 Million Votes in One Day, California Struggles
thread.title_full: Elon Musk: India Counts 640 Million Votes in One Day, California Struggles

author: Siddharth Shankar
title: Elon Musk: India Counts 640 Million Votes in One Day, California Struggles
text:
[Tech News](https://www.news9live.com/technology/tech-news)
Elon Musk: India Counts 640 Million Votes in One Day, California Struggles
Billionaire Elon Musk compared India’s swift vote counting system to California's delayed election results, calling out the state for its inefficiency. India counts 640 million votes in a day using EVMs, while California’s mail-in ballots take weeks to process. The comparison has sparked discussions on election technology and transparency worldwide.
Billionaire Elon Musk stirred up a debate online, highlighting India’s incredible vote-counting speed compared to California’s slow tallying process in the US presidential election. Musk’s comment came as California’s election results remain undecided even after 19 days. He called attention to India’s impressive feat of counting 640 million votes in just one day during its Lok Sabha elections.
Why Did Musk Compare California and India?
California’s election process relies heavily on mail-in ballots, which take longer to process and validate. In contrast, India uses Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs), which make counting votes faster and more streamlined. Musk, frustrated by California’s delayed results, retweeted, “India counted 640 million votes in 1 day. California is still counting votes,” along with a facepalm emoji.
India counted 640 million votes in 1 day.
California is still counting votes 🤦♂️
[https://t.co/ai8JmWxas6]— Elon Musk (@elonmusk)
[November 24, 2024]
Kamala Harris, the Democratic candidate, was declared the winner in California by news agencies, but an official announcement is still pending. The delay stems from California’s election rules that allow until December 1 to “fix” ballots with errors, such as missing signatures.
Meanwhile, Musk’s comment went viral, sparking discussions about the efficiency of voting systems worldwide.
India’s Voting and Counting Process: How Does It Work?
India’s Lok Sabha elections are among the largest democratic exercises in the world. Let’s break it down:
- Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs):
Introduced in 2000, EVMs make counting quick and reliable. These machines work alongside the Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT), which produces a paper slip to confirm each vote. - Decentralized Counting:
Votes are counted simultaneously across 543 constituencies. The process begins with postal ballots before moving to EVMs. - Transparency Measures:
To ensure fairness, five EVMs per constituency are randomly selected for verification with VVPAT slips. This cross-checking ensures the accuracy of results. - Speedy Results:
India’s Supreme Court mandates that postal ballots be counted first to avoid delays. With 14 EVMs processed in each counting hall, results are typically declared by evening.
The US Process: Why So Slow?
Unlike India’s centralized and tech-driven system, California uses mail-in ballots. Each ballot undergoes meticulous checks for signatures, proper envelopes, and other details. This manual process eats up time, especially when millions of votes are involved.
As of now, 98% of the votes are counted in California, and news outlets have called the race for Kamala Harris with 58.6% of the vote. Still, the official announcement remains pending.
Musk’s comment brought global attention to the differences in election systems. While India relies on technology to speed up vote counting, the US prioritizes manual validation to ensure accuracy.
